flashcards.front: Which V refers to the massive scale of Big Data?
flashcards.back: Volume - Refers to the enormous size of datasets exceeding terabytes/petabytes in scale.
The speed at which data is generated/processed (e.g., social media streams, IoT sensors).

flashcards.front: Name three types of data Variety

flashcards.back: Data quality and reliability issues including noise, inconsistencies, and trustworthiness.

flashcards.front: What makes Value the most crucial V?
flashcards.back: It determines the worth of data extraction through meaningful insights and business impact.
Distributed systems like Hadoop HDFS for horizontal scaling across clusters.

flashcards.front: Give an example of Velocity processing
flashcards.back: Real-time stock trading systems analyzing market data streams.
Requires flexible schemas (NoSQL) and format-agnostic storage solutions.
1. Missing values 2. Duplicate records 3. Measurement errors
Effective analytics methods and alignment with organizational goals.
Global datasphere exceeding 1 trillion gigabytes (2023 stats)
Both batch (Volume) and speed (Velocity) layers for comprehensive processing
Variety and Veracity through cleaning/transformation pipelines
Value characteristic through cost-benefit analysis of data initiatives
